questionText: Provide details on the electricity and heat your organization has generated and consumed for cement production activities.
questionKo: details on the electricity 및 heat 귀사 has generated 및 consumed for 시멘트 생산 활동.을/를 입력하십시오.
simpleHelp: Question 7.30.9 has been modified and represented here for the cement sector. This enables consistency of reporting across organizations with varying coverage over activities that may be separate from the cement sector or independent of the production activities defining the cement sector.

columns.text: Total gross generation (MWh) inside the cement sector boundary
columns.textKo: Total gross generation (MWh) inside the cement sector boundary

columns.text: Generation that is consumed (MWh) inside the cement sector boundary
columns.textKo: Generation that is consumed (MWh) inside the cement sector boundary

guidance.text: This question only appears if you select "Generation of electricity, heat, steam, or cooling" in response to 7.30.
guidance.textKo: This 문항 only appears 만약 you select "Generation of electricity, heat, steam, 또는 cooling" in response to 7.30.

guidance.text:
General 
This question is based on question 7.30.9 but is sector specific. Energy carriers generated from outside the cement sector should not be reported here.
The boundary surrounding your organization in the cement sector shall hereby be referred to as the organizational/sector boundary. The sector boundary for energy consumption should align with the sector boundary for emissions, which is described in the guidance to question 7.19.
Figures you provide should be for the reporting year only (as defined by your answer to 1.4).
Guidance on unit conversion is available in the following Technical Note: " Conversion of fuel data to MWh ".
Nuclear power generation should not be included for this question, as nuclear power is covered in more detail in questions for electric utilities.
You should only account for heat generated in transferable mediums, i.e. the forms of heat that may also be purchased or acquired from third parties (as listed in question 7.30.1).
This is because the proportion of fuel combustion heat made available for use in applications after losses (e.g. direct heating for industrial process plant and equipment) may be difficult to measure or would require detailed process monitoring equipment readings.
Total gross generation inside the cement sector boundary (column 2) 
Gross generation should be reported, where ‘Gross' covers the total output from all generating installations or facilities without deducting for electricity, steam, heat or cooling used by the generating plant or facility for the purpose of generation. This includes both the energy you produced and did not consume, as well as the amount you did consume.
Generation that is consumed inside the cement sector boundary (column 3) 
This column is a subset of column 2; the amount entered cannot be higher than the amount entered in column 2. If the entered amount is equal to the amount in column 2, then your organization consumed in the cement sector all of the electricity, steam, and/or heat that your organization generated in the cement sector.
guidance.textKo: 일반 This 문항 is based on 문항 7.30.9 but is sector specific. Energy carriers generated from outside the cement sector 해야 합니다 not be 보고 here. The boundary surrounding 귀사 in the cement sector shall hereby be referred to as the organizational/sector boundary. The sector boundary for 에너지 소비량 해야 합니다 align with the sector boundary for emissions, which is 설명d in the guidance to 문항 7.19. Figures you provide 해야 합니다 be 에 대한 보고연도 only (as defined by your answer to 1.4). Guidance on unit conversion is available in the following Technical Note: " Conversion of fuel data to MWh ". Nuclear 발전 해야 합니다 not be included for this 문항, as nuclear power is covered in more detail in 문항 for electric utilities. You 해야 합니다 only account for heat generated in transferable mediums, i.e. the forms of heat that may also be purchased 또는 acquired from third parties (as listed in 문항 7.30.1). This is because the proportion of fuel combustion heat made available for use in applications after losses (e.g. direct heating for industrial 프로세스 plant 및 equipment) may be difficult to measure 또는 would require detailed 프로세스 모니터링 equipment readings. Total gross generation inside the cement sector boundary (열 2) Gross generation 해야 합니다 be 보고, 여기서 ‘Gross' covers the total output from all generating installations 또는 facilities without deducting for electricity, steam, heat 또는 cooling used by the generating plant 또는 facility 에 대한 purpose of generation. This includes both the energy you produced 및 did not consume, as well as the amount you did consume. Generation that is consumed inside the cement sector boundary (열 3) This 열 is a subset of 열 2; the amount entered cannot be higher than the amount entered in 열 2. 만약 the entered amount is equal to the amount in 열 2, then 귀사 consumed in the cement sector all 의 electricity, steam, 및/또는 heat that 귀사 generated in the cement sector.

guidance.text: Gross generation : covers the total output from all generating installations or facilities without deducting for amount of generated electricity, steam, heat or cooling used by those installations or facilities for the purpose of generation. Deducting this self-consumption of output gives the net generation. To avoid double-counting, consumption of one energy carrier (i.e. electricity, heat, steam, or cooling) to produce another (i.e. electricity, heat, steam, or cooling) within the same installation should not be included. For example, the generation of steam which is consumed in a steam turbine for the generation of electricity should not be included.
guidance.textKo: Gross generation: covers the total output from all generating installations 또는 facilities without deducting for amount of generated electricity, steam, heat 또는 cooling used by those installations 또는 facilities 에 대한 purpose of generation. Deducting this self-consumption of output gives the net generation. To avoid double-counting, consumption of one energy carrier (i.e. electricity, heat, steam, 또는 cooling) to produce another (i.e. electricity, heat, steam, 또는 cooling) within the same installation 해야 합니다 not be included. For example, the generation of steam which is consumed in a steam turbine 에 대한 generation of electricity 해야 합니다 not be included.

criteria.criteria:
Points will be awarded per completed row in proportion to the number of rows disclosed. Partially completed rows will not receive full points.
A maximum of 6/6 points is available for this question.
criteria.criteriaKo: 점수가 부여됩니다 per 완성 행 in proportion to the number of 행 공개. 부분적으로 완성된 행은 최고점을 받을 수 없습니다. 이 문항에서 획득 가능한 최고점은 6/6점입니다.

criteria.criteria:
Table completed - 1 point
A maximum of 1/1 point is available for this question.
criteria.criteriaKo: Table 완성 - 1 점 이 문항에서 획득 가능한 최고점은 1/1점입니다.

simpleHelpKo: 문항 7.30.9 has been 수정 및 represented here 에 대한 cement sector. This enables consistency of reporting across organizations with varying coverage over activities that may be separate from the cement sector 또는 independent 의 production activities defining the cement sector.
